steps.title: Set up the ratio as an equation
steps.content: The ratio of t to u is 1 to 2, which means: $$\frac{t}{u} = \frac{1}{2}$$

steps.title: Substitute t = 10 and solve for u
steps.content: Substitute $t = 10$ into the equation: $$\frac{10}{u} = \frac{1}{2}$$ Cross-multiply to isolate u: $$10 \cdot 2 = 1 \cdot u$$ $$20 = u$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
